6.anchor: My heart sank at his departure.
6.note: Or heart had gone out to him when he spoke

1.note: Probably a member of the crocus family

10.note: Or interior lovingly inlaid / by the daughters of Jerusalem. / Come out, you daughters of Zion, / and look

1.note: The main male and female speakers (identified primarily on the basis of the gender of the relevant Hebrew forms) are indicated by the captions He She Friends.

4.anchor: We rejoice and delight in you

5.anchor: like the tent curtains of Solomon.

6.note: Or fire, / like the very flame of the Lord

11.note: That is, about 25 pounds or about 12 kilograms; also in verse 12

12.note: That is, about 5 pounds or about 2.3 kilograms

9.anchor: flowing gently over lips and teeth.
9.note: Septuagint, Aquila, Vulgate and Syriac; Hebrew lips of sleepers

11.anchor: let us spend the night in the villages.

4.note: The meaning of the Hebrew for this phrase is uncertain.

12.anchor: my desire set me among the royal chariots of my people.
12.note: Or among the chariots of Amminadab among the chariots of the people of the prince

13.anchor: as on the dance of Mahanaim?
13.note: In Hebrew texts this verse (6:13) is numbered 7:1.
